findings.summary: IC1 VCC pin (pin 20) placed on GND net instead of +5V net; IC1 GND pin (pin 10) isolated in unnamed net instead of resolved to GND; SPI not detected despite AVR-ISP-6 header with MISO/MOSI/SCK conn...

correct.description: In the nets section, IC1 pin 20 (named 'VCC', type power_in) is listed as a member of the 'GND' net. In the schematic, IC1 VCC connects via wire to the +5V rail through #PWR07 (+5V at position 5150,2550). The net resolver has miswired this power pin. As a consequence, power_domains for IC1 only shows 'GND' in power_rails and omits '+5V', making the power domain analysis incorrect.

IC1 pin 10 (named 'GND', type power_in) appears only in '__unnamed_3' with point_count=1, not connected to the main 'GND' net. In the schematic, pin 10 connects via wire (3900,3100 → 3900,2300) then through RESET pull-up wiring back to #PWR04 GND symbol at (5150,5100) via a wire at line 403 (5150,4900 → 5150,5100). The GND pin of IC1 should resolve to the main 'GND' net.

The schematic uses text labels PB0-PB7 and PD0-PD6 placed on the right-side connector breakout wires. However, the analyzer assigns these labels to IC1 pins whose library pin names disagree: e.g., net 'PB7' contains IC1 pin 2 which is named '(RXD)PD0', and net 'PD0' contains IC1 pin 19 named '(PCINT7/USCK/SCK/SCL)PB7'. The port B and port D labels are inverted relative to the ATtiny2313 pin functions. This is faithfully reproduced from the source schematic wiring, so the analyzer is technically correct, but downstream bus analysis using pin names (e.g., the I2C detection on 'PD2'/'PD0' nets) is misled because the net names don't match actual port assignments.

missed.description: The ISP header CON1 (AVR-ISP-6) has explicit MISO (pin 1), SCK (pin 3), and MOSI (pin 4) signals. These connect via the RESET pull-up wiring network and unnamed nets to the ATtiny2313 pins PB5/MOSI, PB6/MISO, and PB7/SCK (pins 17, 18, 19). The signal_analysis.spi array is empty [] despite clearly having an SPI ISP programming interface. The ISP-6 header is a standard AVR SPI programming connector.
